findings.title: detect() falls back to port 80 when URL has no explicit port, even for https://
findings.description: In detect() and listModels(), the http.get call uses port || 80, which means a URL like LMSTUDIO_BASE_URL=https://remote.example.com/v1 (no port) would probe http://remote.example.com:80 rather than https://remote.example.com:443. buildRequest() correctly handles HTTPS (falls back to 443 for https:), but the probe methods do not. This creates a false negative for HTTPS-based remote endpoints.
findings.impact: Out of scope for this increment — spec explicitly targets local-first backends (localhost:1234, localhost:8080). Remote HTTPS usage is not a supported path. Surfacing as a LOW for future awareness only.
findings.recommendation: If HTTPS remote support becomes a goal, switch detect/listModels to use the same protocol-aware branch as buildRequest, or use a shared helper. Defer to a follow-up increment.

recommendations: No blocking action required — closure may proceed.
Consider opening a follow-up increment for HTTPS remote endpoint support across all provider probe methods (not just lmstudio/llamacpp — ollama has the same issue).
notes: Scope strictly limited to the 0006 increment changes. The anymodel repo also has uncommitted tool-compressor work (providers/tool-compressor.mjs, proxy.mjs, providers/ollama.mjs, test/optimizations.test.mjs) that was NOT reviewed as it is out of this increment's scope.
Test suite: 239/239 pass (44 suites, ~115ms). No regressions.
